pages.text: A bare stone isle with a cobblestone band — no trees, little grass. Built for mining.

$(bold)Ores$()$(br)A mining isle through and through. Iron shows on most — roughly four in five — in a handful of small veins, with copper, coal and a little lapis, and gold deeper down. Which ores you get depends on where it forms (next page); nothing is guaranteed, and a lean isle happens.

$(bold)Height is everything$()$(br)A Rocky isle takes after the altitude it grows at, like the real underground — so aim your throw.$(br)$(li)High up: coal and iron, with a chance of $(item)emerald$() — snow-capped if very high or grown in the cold.$(li)Mid-air: the broad spread — iron, copper, coal, lapis, gold.$(li)Deep, near the world's floor: $(item)deepslate$(), rich in $(item)diamond$(), redstone, lapis and gold.
